technique: Oil on poplar panel (reproduced here on a playing card!)
description: Here she is, the world's most famous half-smile, riding shotgun on the Ace of Spades! A woman sits turned three-quarters toward us, hands resting calmly one over the other, draped in dark robes with a sheer veil over her hair. Behind her stretches a dreamy, hazy landscape of winding paths, misty mountains and a distant river that famously doesn't quite line up on the left and right sides (Leonardo's sneaky little optical mischief). The whole thing glows with that soft, smoky 'sfumato' blur, like she's lit by candlelight at golden hour. And yes, those eyes really do seem to follow you around the room.
ambiance: Calm, mysterious and quietly amused — like she knows a joke she's never going to tell you. On a playing card she becomes the ultimate winning hand: pull this Ace and you've literally got the Louvre's biggest star in your pocket.
